---
title: Getting started with R
description: An introductory course to programming in [![](img/logo_r.png){width="1.4em"}](https://www.r-project.org/)
title-block-banner: true
---

:::{.topdef}

R is a free and open-source programming language for statistical computing, modelling, and graphics, with a vast collection of stats-specific packages. It is extremely popular in some academic fields such as statistics, biology, bioinformatics, economy, and linguistics.

This introductory course does not assume any prior knowledge.

:::

<br>
[[Start course ➤](intro_why.qmd)]{.topinline}

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LearningResource",
  "name": "Getting started with R",
  "description": "R is a free and open-source programming language for statistical computing, modelling, and graphics, with a vast collection of stats-specific packages. It is extremely popular in some academic fields such as statistics, biology, bioinformatics, economy, and linguistics.\n\nThis course, which does not require any prior programming knowledge, is an introduction to R. It covers R's IDEs, syntax, data types, indexing, function definition, control flow, packages, data exploration, and plotting.",
  "url": "https://mint.westdri.ca/r/top_intro",
  "learningResourceType": "course",
  "inLanguage": "en",
  "license": "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/",
  "provider": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SFU Research Computing Group"
  },
  "keywords": "R",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Marie-Hélène Burle"
  },
  "educationalLevel": "beginner"
}
</script>
